Caller must be the steward (or an owner of stewardAccount)."}},"title":"SkillDefinitionRegistry","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"NAMES()":{"notice":"Optional `.skill` TLD registry. A `.skill` name binds to exactly one skill."}},"notice":"Versioned, content-addressed taxonomy of \"what is this skill?\" Mirrors `GeoFeatureRegistry` for the skills domain. The on-chain row is a small indexable summary; canonical SKOS triples (synonyms, broader / narrower / related, OASF mapping) live off-chain and are anchored by `ontologyMerkleRoot` (URDNA2015-canonical N-Quads \u2192 keccak256 root). Hierarchical \"skill X is a kind of skill Y\" relationships are NOT stored on chain \u2014 that hierarchy lives in the SKOS graph. Putting it on chain would force a new version every time the upstream taxonomy is re-parented. Each `SkillRecord` version pins:   \u2022 `ontologyMerkleRoot`  \u2014 Merkle root over the canonical SKOS subtree                             for this skill (label, altLabels, broader,                             narrower, related, OASF mapping).   \u2022 `predecessorMerkleRoot` \u2014 root of the prior version (or zero), so                             diffs are auditable across taxonomy refreshes.   \u2022 `metadataURI` carries the JSON-LD blob plus a top-level     `oasfRelease` tag (e.g. \"oasf-0.5.2\") so two stewards re-importing     the same OASF concept on different days produce identical     `conceptHash`. Names (`.skill` and any ancestor) are HANDLES, not canonical truth. v1 adds `bindName` paralleling `GeoFeatureRegistry.bindName` so the `.skill` TLD can alias canonical `skillId`s. The `NAMES` reference is optional \u2014 pass `address(0)` at deploy if `.skill` isn't initialised yet (binds become a no-op revert until the registry is wired). Claim writes do NOT happen here \u2014 see `AgentSkillRegistry`. This contract is the source of truth for \"what is skill S at version V\".","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"src/SkillDefinitionRegistry.sol":"SkillDefinitionRegistry"},"evmVersion":"prague","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":200},"remappings":[":@openzeppelin/contracts/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/contracts/",":account-abstraction/=lib/account-abstraction/contracts/",":erc4626-tests/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/lib/erc4626-tests/",":forge-std/=lib/forge-std/src/",":halmos-cheatcodes/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/lib/halmos-cheatcodes/src/",":openzeppelin-contracts/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/"],"viaIR":true},"sources":{"src/AgentNameRegistry.sol":{"keccak256":"0x320b75377fd5aebbf36f0e3ab6ff01036ce0f548936d69fd6253f0ec0cddaafc","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://fbfaecd9dd57883a9dfe76dd8ef1d1f9d1784ea2b553b1b9812fcee6aa77d2bd","dweb:/ipfs/QmTmwCMGr6b4UMeT7ZHDuwx7PRqR3jxmANghUAiCYQpYJ5"]},"src/AgentRelationship.sol":{"keccak256":"0x79063025928424d79229b22cb8c68783ac9ff3527cb5f2b2a92cf44aaf56a3bf","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://448c147db1a31c6526a37a05fe4be4dbd161984b2832e5ee552dc876c48c1762","dweb:/ipfs/QmYBgjKDKHRXrdLkvK7FZwisnfed4qnf764Atb3YXtmuW3"]},"src/SkillDefinitionRegistry.sol":{"keccak256":"0x8fcd2e3149917e261c5a9db6b7ebbb95860480037a5d26a45ab5b208fa7b8ffe","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://57254f48af7df706ae82d22311a7e4de71d2f1888968170c830137fb49fff7fa","dweb:/ipfs/QmViG5uTgKFqkipecpkoCxtJpHrGjx2g2foziXufzhhX1A"]}},"version":1}

bindName(bytes32,bytes32).details: Mirrors `GeoFeatureRegistry.bindName`. The on-chain canonical      handle is still `skillId`; the name is a developer-friendly      alias (and the source of truth for the SDK's reverse      `nameToSkillId` lookup).

params.namesRegistry: Optional `AgentNameRegistry` address for `.skill`        TLD binds. Pass `address(0)` to defer name wiring.

publish(bytes32,bytes32,address,bytes32,.details: skillId is stable across versions; version monotonically      increments. The first call for a given skillId starts at v1.      Caller must be the steward (or an owner of stewardAccount).

NAMES().notice: Optional `.skill` TLD registry. When the zero address, name         binds revert with `NamesNotConfigured`. Wired on first deploy         that includes the `.skill` root initialisation.

bindName(bytes32,bytes32).notice: Bind a `.skill` name node to this skill. Caller must be the         skill's steward AND the name's owner.

deactivate(bytes32).notice: Mark the latest version of a skill inactive (e.g. taxonomy retirement).

publish(bytes32,bytes32,address,bytes32,.notice: Publish a new version of a skill definition.

setValidity(bytes32,uint64,uint64,uint64.notice: Update the validity window of a published version.

skillForName(bytes32).notice: nameNode → skillId. A `.skill` name binds to exactly one skill.
